summary: The Docker-based reproduction builds Zebra from source, starts zebrad in regtest mode with RPC enabled, and sends 20 concurrent sendrawtransaction requests each carrying an 8 MiB hex payload. The hex decoding allocates memory before the Zcash deserialization layer rejects the invalid transaction. The reproduction measures RSS memory before and after the exploit and confirms a measurable memory increase exceeding the verification threshold.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the zebrad process RSS memory to increase by more than 10 MiB after 20 concurrent sendrawtransaction requests with 8 MiB hex payloads are submitted, proving that hex input is decoded into heap buffers without application-level size validation.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction builds Zebra, starts zebrad with RPC on regtest port 18232, reads the generated cookie for authentication, records baseline RSS memory, sends 20 concurrent sendrawtransaction requests each with an 8 MiB hex payload, and measures the post-exploit RSS memory. The server accepts each request, decodes the full hex string into a Vec<u8> allocation, then returns a deserialization error for the invalid transaction data. The aggregate memory increase across 20 concurrent requests exceeds the 10 MiB verification threshold.
verification_method: The entrypoint script measures the zebrad process RSS memory via ps before and after sending the exploit requests. A memory increase exceeding 10240 KiB (10 MiB) confirms that the oversized hex payloads were allocated in server memory without size-based rejection. The verification distinguishes the memory impact of the exploit from normal node operation by comparing RSS deltas against a fixed threshold.
evidence_produced_by_run: Baseline RSS memory recorded before exploit requests.
20 concurrent sendrawtransaction requests with 8 MiB hex payloads each returned HTTP 200 with deserialization error responses, confirming hex decoding succeeded before transaction validation.
Post-exploit RSS memory showed increase exceeding 10 MiB threshold.
No max_request_body_size configuration exists in zebra-rpc/src/config/rpc.rs or zebra-rpc/src/server.rs.
limitations_or_blockers: Building Zebra from source requires significant time (15-30 minutes) and at least 4 GiB RAM for compilation.
The jsonrpsee implicit 10 MiB request body limit bounds each individual request, so the exploit relies on concurrent connections to amplify memory usage.
The reproduction requires the zebrad binary to start successfully in regtest mode, which depends on system resources.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub, checks out commit 92a4e55f9e702468dbb0d8ec57df3e0c3fdd4ea9 (v4.3.0), and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
